title: carex nigra subsp. alpina
intro: Carex nigra subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Carex genus. It is a perennial species that is native to mountainous regions in Europe, where it thrives in damp, acidic environments. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections and respiratory issues. Alpine Sedge can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and has been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and support the immune system.
